answer:
**Kātyāyana Smṛti, chapter 32, addresses the application of divine ordeals (*divyatva*) in legal disputes involving suspicion (*saṃkā*), breach of trust, partition among heirs, denial of gifts, theft, violence, and similar matters.**[^1]

The chapter opens by prohibiting ordeals for plaintiffs and mandating their use only for the accused by ordeal experts.[^2] It restricts capital ordeals in suspicion cases (e.g., no capital consecrated water; balance and similar non-capital ordeals for those suspected by kings or tainted by slander/robbery).[^3] The operative rule is a graduated scale matching ordeal severity to the disputed loss value (calculated in gold equivalents):

- 100 gold: poison ordeal
- 80 gold: fire ordeal
- 60 gold: water ordeal
- 40 gold: balance ordeal
- 20 or 10 gold: consecrated water
- >5 gold or half thereof: rice grains
- Half again: oath (touching head of son/others)
- Lesser: worldly (*laukika*) procedures.[^4]

This tightens Yājñavalkya Smṛti's broader treatment (ch. 2.95–113), which lists the same five core ordeals (balance, fire, water, poison, sacred libation) for grave accusations when human proof fails, with procedures and restrictions (e.g., balance for vulnerable groups like Brāhmaṇas/children; no ploughshare/poison/balance for debts <1,000 *paṇa*s unless royal/grave).[^5] Kātyāyana adds plaintiff immunity, mandates non-capital ordeals for royal servants/suspicions/partitions/collective acts (consecrated water only), proof for gift-denials/theft/violence even in small amounts, and precise monetary thresholds—restricting harsher ordeals to higher stakes while mandating milder alternatives, thus constraining ordeal deployment overall.[^6]

[^1]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, [ch. 32/translation/](works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/32/translation/#p-c2e27e).
[^2]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, [ch. 32/translation/](works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/32/translation/#p-676d77).
[^3]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, [ch. 32/translation/](works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/32/translation/#p-94d1a0).
[^4]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, [ch. 32/translation/](works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/32/translation/#p-c8377d) (scale from K_418–421).
[^5]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [ch. 2/translation/](works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-e6e133); [ch. 2/translation/](works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-357a68).
[^6]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, [ch. 32/translation/](works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/32/translation/#p-1d5276).
